The classification of Cynanchum haitiense is further supported by its unique characteristics and morphological features.

intro: Cynanchum haitiense, also known as Haiti dogbane, is a plant species within the Apocynaceae family, native to Haiti and the Dominican Republic. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Cynanchum haitiense include alkaloids, such as haitianine and haitiadinine, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infused in water to treat various ailments, and it is also used in the form of tinctures or extracts in modern herbal medicine.
